sections.heading: Surveillance
sections.content: {"clinical_follow_up_schedule": [{"setting": "Post-definitive therapy (RP or RT) - first 5 years", "schedule": "PSA every 6-12 months; consider DRE if suspicion of recurrence"}, {"setting": "Post-definitive therapy - after 5 years", "schedule": "PSA annually; consider DRE if suspicion of recurrence"}, {"setting": "High-risk patients for recurrence", "schedule": "PSA as frequently as every 3 months may be necessary to clarify disease status"}, {"setting": "N1M0 on ADT", "schedule": "Physical examination + PSA every 3-6 months; imaging for symptoms or increasing PSA"}, {"setting": "Localized disease on observation", "schedule": "Physical examination + PSA every 3-6 months; imaging for symptoms or increasing PSA"}, {"setting": "Active surveillance - low risk", "schedule": "PSA no more often than every 6 months; DRE no more often than every 12 months; repeat prostate biopsy no more often than every 12 months; repeat mpMRI no more than every 12 months"}, {"setting": "Active surveillance - favorable intermediate risk", "schedule": "Same as low risk but with greater intensity of monitoring; confirmatory prostate biopsy within 1-2 years of diagnostic biopsy; consider earlier repeat biopsy within 6-12 months if initial biopsy was not mpMRI-guided"}, {"setting": "M0 CRPC", "schedule": "Monitoring with physical exam, PSA every 3-6 months, and imaging for symptoms or increasing PSA; document castrate levels of testosterone"}, {"setting": "M1 CRPC or mCSPC on systemic therapy", "schedule": "Physical examination + PSA every 3-6 months; periodic imaging to monitor treatment response; in CRPC, 8-12 week imaging intervals appear reasonable"}], "imaging_strategy": [{"setting": "Low-risk localized disease", "imaging": "No routine imaging recommended; bone and soft tissue imaging not indicated for asymptomatic patients with PSA <10 ng/mL"}, {"setting": "Unfavorable intermediate-risk, high-risk, very-high-risk localized disease", "imaging": "Bone and soft tissue imaging recommended; PSMA-PET/CT or PSMA-PET/MRI can be considered as alternative to CT, MRI, and bone scans; mpMRI preferred over CT for pelvic staging"}, {"setting": "Post-RP PSA persistence/recurrence", "imaging": "Bone and soft tissue imaging for life expectancy >5 years; PSMA-PET/CT has increased sensitivity at low PSA levels; bone scans rarely positive in asymptomatic patients with PSA <10 ng/mL"}, {"setting": "Post-RT recurrence", "imaging": "PSADT calculation, bone and soft tissue imaging, consideration of prostate/seminal vesicle biopsy if local recurrence suspected"}, {"setting": "Metastatic disease monitoring", "imaging": "Bone scans and soft tissue imaging obtained regularly during systemic therapy; bone scans as often as every 6-12 months for ADT monitoring; 8-12 week imaging intervals in CRPC"}, {"setting": "M1 CRPC treatment selection", "imaging": "PSMA-PET imaging should only be used to determine if patient is a candidate for Lu-177-PSMA-617; changes in systemic therapy should not be made solely based on positive PSMA-PET in M0 CRPC"}], "laboratory_monitoring": ["PSA: cornerstone of monitoring; frequency depends on disease state and risk of recurrence", "PSA doubling time: calculated to inform nomogram use and counseling; relative risk for bone metastasis or death increases as PSADT shortens", "Testosterone levels: document castrate levels (<50 ng/dL) if progression occurs on ADT; monitor testosterone 12 weeks after first dose of LHRH therapy then upon increase in PSA", "Serum calcium and creatinine: required prior to denosumab and zoledronic acid therapy; periodic monitoring with denosumab use", "Vitamin D levels: recommended baseline assessment and monitoring during antiresorptive therapy", "Liver function: monthly monitoring during abiraterone therapy", "Potassium and phosphate: monthly monitoring during abiraterone therapy", "Complete blood count: regular monitoring during chemotherapy and PARP inhibitor therapy", "Hepatic and renal function: monitoring during olaparib and rucaparib therapy with dose adjustments as needed"], "supportive_follow_up": ["DEXA scan before starting ADT and every 1-2 years while on ADT or antiresorptive therapy", "Fracture risk assessment using FRAX annually for all patients on ADT or remaining hypogonadal", "Cardiovascular risk assessment and management of modifiable risk factors", "Geriatric assessment consideration for older adults", "NCCN Distress Thermometer and Problem List utilization including social determinants of health", "Patient-reported outcomes assessment using validated instruments (e.g., EPIC-26)", "Comprehensive dental evaluation before initiating osteoclast inhibitor therapy", "Baseline DEXA and follow-up DEXA after 1 year of antiresorptive therapy", "Consideration of intermittent ADT to reduce toxicity when appropriate"]}

timeline.title: Post-definitive therapy (RP or RT) - first 5 years
timeline.body: PSA every 6-12 months; consider DRE if suspicion of recurrence

timeline.title: Post-definitive therapy - after 5 years
timeline.body: PSA annually; consider DRE if suspicion of recurrence

timeline.title: High-risk patients for recurrence
timeline.body: PSA as frequently as every 3 months may be necessary to clarify disease status

timeline.title: N1M0 on ADT
timeline.body: Physical examination + PSA every 3-6 months; imaging for symptoms or increasing PSA

timeline.title: Localized disease on observation
timeline.body: Physical examination + PSA every 3-6 months; imaging for symptoms or increasing PSA

timeline.title: Active surveillance - low risk
timeline.body: PSA no more often than every 6 months; DRE no more often than every 12 months; repeat prostate biopsy no more often than every 12 months; repeat mpMRI no more than every 12 months

timeline.title: Active surveillance - favorable intermediate risk
timeline.body: Same as low risk but with greater intensity of monitoring; confirmatory prostate biopsy within 1-2 years of diagnostic biopsy; consider earlier repeat biopsy within 6-12 months if initial biopsy was not mpMRI-guided

timeline.title: M0 CRPC
timeline.body: Monitoring with physical exam, PSA every 3-6 months, and imaging for symptoms or increasing PSA; document castrate levels of testosterone

timeline.title: M1 CRPC or mCSPC on systemic therapy
timeline.body: Physical examination + PSA every 3-6 months; periodic imaging to monitor treatment response; in CRPC, 8-12 week imaging intervals appear reasonable
